items.rationale: AGENTS.md is comprehensive and recently maintained (CLAUDE.md symlinks to it), but its platform-command list still says `tags` — the command was renamed to `topics` with a `tags` alias (ENG-562) — and omits the new `audit` command. Refresh the command inventory only; preserve the curated packaging, contract-discipline, and release sections.

README's platform-command table lists `dosu tags` but the command is now `dosu topics` (with `tags` kept as an alias), and the table is missing the new `dosu audit` command (agent-audit-driven doc generation). The release-table version examples (0.20.1) are also several minors behind the published 0.29.x.

No architecture doc exists, and the repo has clear layering worth documenting: entry point src/index.ts -> src/cli/cli.ts, with distinct modules for auth (browser OAuth + ticket flow), client (typed tRPC over a vendored generated contract with CI sync gates), config, mcp (provider factory system for 16 AI tools), commands, setup wizard, agent mode, hooks (agent hot-path), tui, and version/update checks. Org knowledge already documents the CLI Contract System and post-auth onboarding UX in depth — the architecture doc should mirror those, especially the vendored-contract boundary (src/generated/dosu-api-types.d.ts).

No deps.md, and the dependency policy is unusual enough to warrant one: all 17 deps live in devDependencies with zero runtime dependencies because bun build --target node inlines everything into the published bin/dosu.js bundle; bun.lock is committed and bunfig.toml enforces minimumReleaseAge=259200 (3-day supply-chain delay). A deps doc should capture this policy plus the per-dependency purposes.
